themes.name: National Resilience and Historical Continuity
themes.reasoning: The President draws a direct line from the 1956 Women's March and the 1976 Soweto Uprising to the current spirit of the nation, suggesting that historical courage is the foundation for overcoming modern challenges.

themes.name: Economic Recovery and Stabilization
themes.reasoning: A significant portion of the speech highlights positive economic indicators such as GDP growth, budget surpluses, lower inflation, and the performance of the JSE as evidence of a successful turnaround strategy.

themes.name: Safety and the Fight Against Organised Crime
themes.reasoning: Organised crime is identified as an immediate threat to democracy, prompting a major focus on new law enforcement strategies, military support, and intelligence-led interventions.

themes.name: Anti-Corruption and Institutional Integrity
themes.reasoning: The speech emphasizes strengthening the rule of law through the Madlanga Commission's findings, vetting processes, and legislative reforms like the Whistle-Blower Protection Bill.

themes.name: Infrastructure and Essential Service Delivery (Water and Local Government)
themes.reasoning: The 'water crisis' and the dysfunction of local municipalities are framed as critical failures that require urgent national intervention, financial incentives, and systemic reform.

themes.name: Collaborative Governance (Government of National Unity)
themes.reasoning: The GNU is presented as a mature political development that enables cooperation across the political spectrum to drive a common growth agenda.

themes.name: Water Infrastructure and Crisis Governance
themes.reasoning: The transcript details a centralized response to the water crisis, including the formation of a National Water Crisis Committee and R54 billion in incentives for municipal reforms.

themes.name: Local Government Accountability and Reform
themes.reasoning: There is a strong focus on addressing municipal dysfunction through a new White Paper, professionalizing staff, and intervening where service delivery fails.

themes.name: Strategic Infrastructure and Economic Growth
themes.reasoning: The government outlines a R1 trillion public investment plan, Operation Vulindlela, and the creation of specialized commercial courts to drive development.

themes.name: Energy Security and Renewable Transition
themes.reasoning: The text emphasizes restructuring Eskom, ending load reduction, and achieving a 40% renewable energy share by 2030.

themes.name: Logistics and Transport Modernization
themes.reasoning: Plans for private rail operators, port partnerships, and high-speed rail corridors are identified as keys to global competitiveness.

themes.name: Agricultural Productivity and Bio-security
themes.reasoning: Focus on supporting farmers with 10,000 extension officers and an urgent national response to the foot-and-mouth disease outbreak.

themes.name: Green Growth and Mining Revitalization
themes.reasoning: Incentivizing new energy vehicles (NEVs) and harnessing critical mineral reserves for global supply chains.

themes.name: Inclusive Growth and Small Business Support
themes.reasoning: Significant funding (R2.5bn) for SMEs and a R2 trillion investment target aimed at job creation and transformation.

themes.name: Employment Stimulus and Social Safety Net
themes.reasoning: Expansion of public employment programs like the Presidential Employment Stimulus to provide work and income support.

themes.name: Education Reform and Skills Revolution
themes.reasoning: Making Grade R compulsory, overhauling the skills development system, and expanding higher education capacity.

themes.name: Education and Skills Transformation
themes.reasoning: The text outlines a 'skills revolution' and a fundamental overhaul of the skills development system, emphasizing TVET colleges, artisan development, and higher education expansion.

themes.name: Public Health and Nutrition
themes.reasoning: There is a major focus on ending child stunting by 2030, tackling malnutrition, rolling out new HIV prevention technologies (Lenacapavir), and investing in health infrastructure.

themes.name: Digital Transformation of Governance
themes.reasoning: The government is prioritizing the launch of a Digital ID and the digitization of various public services (licenses, certificates) to improve efficiency and access via the MyMzansi platform.

themes.name: Governance and SOE Reform
themes.reasoning: The text details efforts to professionalize the public service, implement lifestyle audits, and move toward a centralized model for managing State-Owned Enterprises (SOEs) like Eskom and Transnet.

themes.name: Security and Migration Control
themes.reasoning: Commitments include strengthening border security through technology and personnel, re-developing border posts, and increasing labor inspections to enforce immigration laws.

themes.name: Social Justice and Redress
themes.reasoning: Themes of redressing past injustices are evident in the District Six restitution project and the ongoing response to Gender-Based Violence and Femicide (GBVF).

themes.name: International Relations and Pan-Africanism
themes.reasoning: The text emphasizes South Africa's role in the AU, SADC, and G20, advocating for the Global South, continental peace, and sovereignty.

themes.name: National Dialogue and Unity
themes.reasoning: The rollout of a country-wide National Dialogue and a subsequent National Convention highlights a strategy for building national consensus and a common vision.

commitments.description: Deployment of the SANDF to support police in Gauteng and Western Cape.
commitments.reasoning: This is an explicit executive directive to deploy military forces within a specific timeframe to combat gang violence and illegal mining.
commitments.raw_info: I have directed the Minister of Police and the SANDF to develop a tactical plan where our security forces should be deployed within the next few days in the Western Cape and Gauteng to deal with gang violence and illegal mining.

commitments.description: Recruitment of 5,500 additional police officers this year.
commitments.reasoning: A specific, quantifiable target for increasing law enforcement capacity within the current year.
commitments.raw_info: We are putting more boots on the ground through the recruitment this year of 5 500 additional police officers...

commitments.description: Establishment of the National Illicit Economy Disruption Programme.
commitments.reasoning: An explicit commitment to create a new programme utilizing AI and data analytics to target sectors like tobacco and fuel.
commitments.raw_info: We are establishing a National Illicit Economy Disruption Programme that brings together key state agencies and other stakeholders... target high-risk sectors like tobacco, fuel, alcohol and other counterfeit products.

commitments.description: Re-vetting and lifestyle audits of SAPS and Metro Police Senior Management.
commitments.reasoning: A commitment to institutional reform through the State Security Agency to ensure ethical leadership in law enforcement.
commitments.raw_info: The State Security Agency will re-vet the Senior Management of the SAPS and Metro Police departments. The vetting process will include lifestyle audits.

commitments.description: Implementation of reforms based on the Madlanga Commission findings.
commitments.reasoning: A commitment to use the commission's recommendations as a blueprint for fundamental reform of the criminal justice system.
commitments.raw_info: As we have successfully done with previous commissions, we will use the recommendations of the Madlanga Commission to make far-reaching changes.

commitments.description: Creation of a criminal justice reform initiative team in The Presidency.
commitments.reasoning: An organizational commitment to centralize and accelerate reforms against organised crime and corruption.
commitments.raw_info: Drawing on the success of Operation Vulindlela... we will use a similar approach to establish a hard-hitting new criminal justice reform initiative... using a dedicated team in The Presidency.

commitments.description: Finalization of the government's approach to an independent anti-corruption body.
commitments.reasoning: A commitment to resolve the structure of a permanent, overarching anti-corruption entity.
commitments.raw_info: We will finalise government’s approach to the recommendation of the National Anti-Corruption Advisory Council on the establishment of a permanent, independent, overarching anti-corruption body.

commitments.description: Introduction of the Whistle-Blower Protection Bill in Parliament.
commitments.reasoning: An explicit legislative commitment to protect and support individuals who report corruption.
commitments.raw_info: The Whistle-Blower Protection Bill will be introduced in Parliament. Among other things, this will criminalise retaliation and provide psychosocial, legal and financial support to whistle-blowers.

commitments.description: Finalization of new Public Procurement Act regulations by mid-2026.
commitments.reasoning: A specific deadline-bound commitment to reform the procurement system and curb corruption.
commitments.raw_info: Measures will include the use of technology and the finalisation of new Public Procurement Act regulations by mid-2026.

commitments.description: Ministerial intervention and community engagement regarding water shortages.
commitments.reasoning: An immediate directive for high-level officials to address local service delivery failures and communicate with citizens.
commitments.raw_info: I have directed the Minister of Water and Sanitation and her deputies as well as the Minister of Cooperative Governance and Traditional Affairs to attend to the water shortage problem and engage with our communities.

commitments.description: Public funding of R156 billion for water and sanitation over three years.
commitments.reasoning: A multi-year fiscal commitment to upgrade and build water infrastructure.
commitments.raw_info: We have committed more than R156 billion in public funding for water and sanitation infrastructure over the next three years.

commitments.description: Establishment of the National Water Resource Infrastructure Agency.
commitments.reasoning: A commitment to create a new agency to manage and mobilize funding for water infrastructure.
commitments.raw_info: we are in the final stages of establishing a National Water Resource Infrastructure Agency to effectively manage and mobilise funding for the country’s water infrastructure.

commitments.description: Formation and chairing of the National Water Crisis Committee by the President.
commitments.reasoning: An elevation of the water issue to a national priority under the President's direct oversight.
commitments.raw_info: we will now elevate our response to the water crisis to a National Water Crisis Committee, which I will chair.

commitments.description: Laying personal criminal charges against Municipal Managers for water act violations.
commitments.reasoning: An explicit commitment to enforce personal accountability for municipal service delivery failures.
commitments.raw_info: We will now move to lay charges against Municipal Managers in their personal capacity for violating the National Water Act of 1998.

commitments.description: Implementation of a R54 billion incentive for metros to reform services.
commitments.reasoning: A financial commitment aimed at incentivizing structural reforms in local government utilities.
commitments.raw_info: we have introduced a new R54 billion incentive for metros to reform their water, sanitation and electricity services.

commitments.description: Finalization of a revised White Paper on Local Government.
commitments.reasoning: A commitment to overhaul the policy framework for local governance in the coming months.
commitments.raw_info: we will, in the coming months finalise a revised White Paper on Local Government. This will provide solutions for the functioning of an effective local government system.

commitments.description: Ensuring independent appointment of qualified senior local government officials.
commitments.reasoning: A commitment to professionalize local government by removing political interference from the hiring process.
commitments.raw_info: We will ensure that senior officials in local government have the required qualifications and are appointed through an independent process free from political interference.

commitments.description: Strengthening the ability of national government to intervene in failing municipalities.
commitments.reasoning: An implicit commitment to legislative or procedural changes that allow faster corrective action by the state.
commitments.raw_info: Where municipalities fail, we will strengthen the ability of national government to intervene more quickly and to direct corrective measures...

commitments.description: Streamlining gun legislation and increasing enforcement of gun laws.
commitments.reasoning: An explicit commitment to tackle gun crime through legislative and enforcement measures.
commitments.raw_info: We are going to tackle gun crime by streamlining legislation and regulations on licencing, possessing and trading in firearms and ammunition. We will increase enforcement of existing gun laws.

commitments.description: Establishment and chairing of the National Water Crisis Committee
commitments.reasoning: Explicit statement: 'National Water Crisis Committee, which I will chair.'
commitments.raw_info: response to the water crisis to a National Water Crisis Committee, which I will chair.

commitments.description: Criminal charges against Municipal Managers in their personal capacity
commitments.reasoning: Explicit intent to hold individuals accountable for water act violations.
commitments.raw_info: We will now move to lay charges against Municipal Managers in their personal capacity for violating the National Water Act of 1998.

commitments.description: R54 billion incentive for metros to reform water, sanitation, and electricity services
commitments.reasoning: A specific financial commitment linked to local government reform.
commitments.raw_info: we have introduced a new R54 billion incentive for metros to reform their water, sanitation and electricity services.

commitments.description: Finalization of a revised White Paper on Local Government
commitments.reasoning: A time-bound commitment to policy overhaul.
commitments.raw_info: in the coming months finalise a revised White Paper on Local Government.

commitments.description: Establishment of specialized courts for commercial matters
commitments.reasoning: Commitment to legal infrastructure reform to support the economy.
commitments.raw_info: we will establish specialised courts for commercial matters with dedicated judges and dedicated court rolls

commitments.description: Establishment of a professional State Property Company
commitments.reasoning: Commitment to create a new entity this year.
commitments.raw_info: This year, we will begin work to establish a professional State Property Company

commitments.description: Target of 40% renewable energy by 2030
commitments.reasoning: A specific, measurable long-term energy target.
commitments.raw_info: By 2030, more than 40% of our energy supply will come from cheap, clean and renewable energy sources.

commitments.description: Eradicating load reduction by next year
commitments.reasoning: A time-bound goal to fix localized power distribution issues.
commitments.raw_info: with the objective of eradicating load reduction by next year.

commitments.description: Initiation of major public-private partnerships in port terminals and rail corridors
commitments.reasoning: Commitment to a specific economic model later this year.
commitments.raw_info: Later this year, we will initiate major public-private partnerships in our port terminals and rail corridors

commitments.description: Issuing a Request For Proposals (RFP) for high-speed rail corridors
commitments.reasoning: A concrete step in a long-term infrastructure project.
commitments.raw_info: We are preparing to send out a Request For Proposals, which will introduce a new era of long-distance rail travel

commitments.description: Deployment of 10,000 new Extension Officers to support farmers
commitments.reasoning: A specific human resource commitment to the agricultural sector.
commitments.raw_info: We will deploy 10 000 new Extension Officers to support farmers and improve agricultural productivity.

commitments.description: Vaccinating the national herd of 14 million cattle (28 million vaccines)
commitments.reasoning: A massive, resource-heavy bio-security commitment within a 12-month timeframe.
commitments.raw_info: We have decided to vaccinate the national herd of 14 million cattle. This requires 28 million vaccines over the next 12 months.

commitments.description: Expansion of the Electronic Travel Authorisation System to all visa-requiring countries
commitments.reasoning: Commitment to digitalize tourism access in the coming year.
commitments.raw_info: In the coming year, we will extend the Electronic Travel Authorisation System to all countries that require a visa

commitments.description: 150% tax deduction for investment in new energy vehicles (NEVs)
commitments.reasoning: A specific fiscal incentive starting in March.
commitments.raw_info: From March this year, we will introduce a 150% tax deduction for investment in new energy vehicles

commitments.description: Target of R2 trillion in new investments over five years
commitments.reasoning: A high-level measurable investment goal.
commitments.raw_info: We have now set ourselves a target of raising R2 trillion in new investments over the next five years.

commitments.description: R2.5 billion in funding for over 180,000 small and medium enterprises
commitments.reasoning: A specific budget allocation for the current year.
commitments.raw_info: This year, we will provide more than R2.5 billion in funding to over 180 000 small and medium enterprises

commitments.description: Expansion of public employment programmes (CWP, EPWP, and Stimulus)
commitments.reasoning: Commitment to grow social employment initiatives in the coming year.
commitments.raw_info: In the coming year, we will expand our public employment programmes, including the CWP, EPWP and the Presidential Employment Stimulus.

commitments.description: 7% employment equity and procurement target for persons with disabilities by 2030
commitments.reasoning: A specific, time-bound mandate for transformation.
commitments.raw_info: increase employment equity targets of persons with disabilities in the Public Service to 7% by 2030, and to mandate a 7% preferential procurement target

commitments.description: Making Grade R compulsory for all children
commitments.reasoning: A fundamental shift in basic education policy.
commitments.raw_info: By making Grade R compulsory, we are getting all children off to a good start.

commitments.description: Restoring skills development levy return to employers to 40%
commitments.reasoning: A specific financial policy reform for the skills sector.
commitments.raw_info: increase the proportion of the skills development levy returned to employers, restoring it to its original level of 40%.

commitments.description: Directive to build more universities and TVET colleges with specialized focus
commitments.reasoning: A direct instruction from the President to expand educational infrastructure.
commitments.raw_info: I have directed the Ministers of Finance and Higher Education to work on a proposal to build more universities and TVET colleges

commitments.description: Fundamental overhaul of the skills development system to implement a dual training model.
commitments.reasoning: Explicitly stated as an undertaking to integrate education with practical workplace experience.
commitments.raw_info: We are undertaking a fundamental overhaul of the skills development system to implement a dual training model that integrates education with practical workplace experience.

commitments.description: Reform and reduce the number of Sector Education and Training Authorities (SETAs).
commitments.reasoning: Direct commitment to improve governance and alignment with the economy.
commitments.raw_info: We will reform and reduce the number of Sector Education and Training Authorities to improve governance, strengthen industry participation...

commitments.description: Restore the skills development levy returned to employers to 40%.
commitments.reasoning: A specific fiscal policy commitment for employers.
commitments.raw_info: we will increase the proportion of the skills development levy returned to employers, restoring it to its original level of 40%.

commitments.description: Build more universities and TVET colleges.
commitments.reasoning: The President issued a directive to ministers to work on this proposal.
commitments.raw_info: I have directed the Ministers of Finance and Higher Education to work on a proposal to build more universities and TVET colleges...

commitments.description: Address the shortage of student accommodation.
commitments.reasoning: Direct instruction to the Minister of Higher Education to work with financial institutions.
commitments.raw_info: I have directed the Minister of Higher Education to address this challenge, working with financial institutions...

commitments.description: End child stunting by 2030.
commitments.reasoning: A time-bound goal aligned with the National Strategy to Accelerate Action for Children.
commitments.raw_info: This year, we will embark on a mission to end child stunting by 2030 and tackle malnutrition among young children...

commitments.description: Allocate clear funding for child nutrition in the Medium-Term Budget Policy Statement.
commitments.reasoning: Explicit commitment to budgetary transparency for this priority.
commitments.raw_info: The Medium-Term Budget Policy Statement will set out a clear allocation for the actions we need to take [regarding stunting].

commitments.description: Propose and consult on measures to curb excessive alcohol use (e.g., minimum pricing).
commitments.reasoning: Commitment to regulatory intervention in public health.
commitments.raw_info: we have proposed measures to curb excessive alcohol use, including minimum unit pricing or higher excise duties...

commitments.description: Continue and redesign the Social Relief of Distress (SRD) Grant.
commitments.reasoning: Commitment to maintain the safety net while improving its link to work opportunities.
commitments.raw_info: this grant will be continued. This year, we will redesign the grant to more effectively support livelihoods...

commitments.description: Expand affordable housing and revitalise commuter rail.
commitments.reasoning: Ongoing commitment to reducing the cost of living for working families.
commitments.raw_info: We are re-making our cities... by expanding affordable housing and revitalising commuter rail.

commitments.description: Proceed with Phase 4 of District Six construction with R500 million allocation.
commitments.reasoning: Specific infrastructure and restitution commitment with allocated budget.
commitments.raw_info: With R500 million allocated to this work, we are proceeding with Phase 4 of construction [District Six].

commitments.description: Massive rollout of Lenacapavir (six-monthly HIV prevention injection).
commitments.reasoning: Health intervention commitment aimed at eliminating HIV transmission.
commitments.raw_info: we will be undertaking a massive rollout of Lenacapavir, a six-monthly injection that has proven highly effective...

commitments.description: Ensure HPV vaccine access for every girl between ages 9 and 15.
commitments.reasoning: Public health goal to end cervical cancer.
commitments.raw_info: mobilising society to ensure that every young girl between the ages of 9 and 15 receives the HPV vaccine.

commitments.description: Hire an additional 10,000 labour inspectors this year.
commitments.reasoning: Specific recruitment target to tighten enforcement of labor and immigration laws.
commitments.raw_info: To tighten enforcement, we will hire an additional 10 000 labour inspectors this year.

commitments.description: Prioritize funding for border security infrastructure and technology (drones).
commitments.reasoning: Explicit commitment to national security and border integrity.
commitments.raw_info: Funding to strengthen border security will be prioritised, covering infrastructure, technology and people.

commitments.description: Launch a Digital ID this year.
commitments.reasoning: Department of Home Affairs (DHA) project with a 2024/2025 deadline.
commitments.raw_info: This year, the DHA will launch a Digital ID to enable safe and secure use of digital services...

commitments.description: Digitise driver's licenses, Matric certificates, and Master's Office services.
commitments.reasoning: Specific service delivery improvement via digital transformation.
commitments.raw_info: We will digitise driver’s licenses, Matric certificates and services at the Master’s Office.

commitments.description: Finalise the National State Enterprises Bill.
commitments.reasoning: Legislative commitment to reform the governance of SOEs.
commitments.raw_info: This includes finalising the National State Enterprises Bill in line with this approach.

commitments.description: Withdraw South African troops from the UN Mission in the DRC.
commitments.reasoning: Formal request made to the UN to consolidate domestic defense resources.
commitments.raw_info: We have requested the UN to allow us to withdraw our troops from the UN Mission in the Democratic Republic of the Congo...

commitments.description: Spread the National Dialogue process to every community and sector.
commitments.reasoning: Commitment to broad-based civic engagement leading to a national compact.
commitments.raw_info: This year, the National Dialogue process will spread across South Africa, reaching every community...

commitments.description: Convene a National Convention to guide the National Development Plan beyond 2030.
commitments.reasoning: The culminating event of the National Dialogue process.
commitments.raw_info: These public dialogues will culminate in a National Convention where all of these conversations will be brought together...

commitments.description: Fix local government and restore trust in the criminal justice system by 2026.
commitments.reasoning: Identified as mandatory pillars for a 'transformative change' year in 2026.
commitments.raw_info: the year 2026 must be a year of transformative change. ... We must fix local government. We must fight crime, corruption...

commitments.description: Establish a central registry for disciplinary cases across government.
commitments.reasoning: Governance commitment to prevent re-employment of offenders in the state.
commitments.raw_info: We are establishing a central registry for disciplinary cases across all spheres of government.

commitments.description: Launch the International Panel on Inequality with international partners.
commitments.reasoning: Global advocacy commitment arising from the G20 presidency.
commitments.raw_info: working with our international partners towards launching the International Panel on Inequality.
